summary: Review #2 of CC-0098 fix-up cycle addressing external reviewer berendt's review #1 (W-001..W-004, I-001, I-002). Changes: (1) reconcile_config.go now gates the LoggingStderrDisabled Warning event on a state transition into a new informational LoggingHealthy=False/StderrDisabled status condition via the new recordLoggingHealth helper, eliminating per-reconcile event flooding (CC-0091 pattern); (2) reconcile_config_test.go extends the REQ-003 test to assert the rendered ConfigMap carries use_stderr=false alongside the Warning event, and adds three new tests covering transition gating, the True/StderrEnabled happy path, and the back-transition; (3) reconcile_deployment_test.go replaces brittle absolute-index pins for --log-master/--log-format with relative-order assertions; (4) chainsaw-test.yaml adds a JSONFormatter contract-key check (name/levelname/message) per record after the parseability check; (5) docs/keystone-events.md catalogues the LoggingStderrDisabled event with its transition-gating note and adds it to the call graph; (6) docs/keystone-reconciler.md adds a renderLoggingConf template-deviation section explaining why [loggers] declares only the root logger and why [handler_stderr] pins level=NOTSET. The architecture submodule cross-link (W-001 from external review) was already addressed by the prior chore commit 6618a8ca on this branch. All tests pass; go vet and go build clean. The new LoggingHealthy condition is a deliberate divergence from the original plan's 'no new condition' constraint, requested by the external reviewer and applied as informational (not Ready-blocking) per REQ-007's spirit.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/... — all green, including 3 new gating/transition tests)
Edge cases are covered: first-reconcile transition fires event; second reconcile suppresses event (gating); back-transition to True does not emit event; happy path writes True/StderrEnabled
REQ-003 test now asserts rendered key (use_stderr=false in keystone.conf) AND the Warning event, closing the W-003 gap
Chainsaw JSON step asserts oslo.log JSONFormatter contract keys (name/levelname/message), closing the W-004 gap
uwsgi flag-order test uses relative-position assertions (mirrors sibling test) instead of brittle absolute indices

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns: condition+event emission gated on transition mirrors the CC-0091 pattern documented in .planwerk/review_patterns/match-condition-reason-to-emitted-event-on-state-transitions.md
Constants extracted: conditionTypeLoggingHealthy, conditionReasonStderrDisabled, conditionReasonStderrEnabled — no inline string literals
recordLoggingHealth has a substantive package-internal docstring explaining the gating contract and the upsert-on-every-reconcile policy
Comment on the [logger_keystone] structural assertion was reworked from an incorrect Python-logging-precedence rationale to a correct single-source-of-truth structural argument

security_checklist.item: No new external inputs introduced; merged config is internal-only state already validated by existing webhook
Event/condition messages use %q for the user-controlled use_stderr value, preventing format-string injection

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible given the constraint: one helper, one condition, three constants, gated-event invariant locked in by tests

LoggingHealthy is intentionally NOT in subConditionTypes — preserves REQ-007's intent that logging is not Ready-blocking even though a new condition was introduced (deviation justified by W-002 reviewer feedback and explicitly documented in code comment at reconcile_config.go:24-31)
No new Service, Mount, or sidecar; LoggingHealthy is status-only and surfaces only via kubectl describe
uwsgiCommand changes remain LoggingSpec-independent — no spec threaded through; flags added unconditionally for both text and json

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No code duplication: the condition/event constants live next to their consumer; recordLoggingHealth is the single owner of LoggingHealthy
No speculative future-proofing: only the StderrDisabled failure mode is tracked, with a comment noting it is the only currently-tracked mode

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected early at admission via existing webhook for spec.logging.format/level/perLoggerLevels enums (unchanged by this PR)
use_stderr=false override is loud-but-honoured: warning event on transition + persistent LoggingHealthy=False condition surfaces the misconfiguration without breaking the escape-hatch contract

defensive_checklist.item: merged["DEFAULT"] nil check before key access prevents nil-map panic
prev-pointer read happens before SetCondition slice mutation, avoiding any aliasing pitfall

suggested_improvements: Optional: document the new LoggingHealthy informational status condition in docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md (the file currently shows Ready and KeystoneAPIReady in its example status block but has no comprehensive condition table). Adding a one-line entry pointing readers at docs/reference/keystone/keystone-events.md#logging would close the discoverability loop. Not a blocker — keystone-events.md already covers the user-visible behavior end-to-end.

Optional: recordLoggingHealth currently takes the full merged map but only reads merged["DEFAULT"]["use_stderr"]. Passing the resolved string directly would tighten the contract, but the current shape keeps the call site readable and the helper reusable should additional [DEFAULT] keys ever join the gated set.
next_steps: Merge — all external-reviewer findings (W-001..W-004, I-001, I-002) are addressed, all unit tests pass, and no new defects were introduced by the fix-up cycle.

detected_patterns.name: Gated event emission via informational status-condition transition
detected_patterns.applies_when: Emitting a Warning/Normal event for a steady-state misconfiguration that is purely a function of user spec (so reconcile polling would otherwise re-emit the event indefinitely). Use an informational status condition (not Ready-blocking) as the transition gate.
detected_patterns.description: Maintain a status condition that mirrors the misconfiguration state (Healthy=True/False with stable Reason strings), upsert it on every reconcile so status reflects current state, and only emit the corresponding event when the condition transitions into the failure state (prev==nil || prev.Status!=False || prev.Reason!=expectedReason). The condition is intentionally excluded from subConditionTypes/Ready aggregation so it surfaces the issue without flipping the CR Ready=False — appropriate for explicit operator overrides where the controller honours the override but warns about its impact. Tests must cover four cases: (a) initial transition emits, (b) repeat reconcile suppresses, (c) back-transition to healthy does not emit a recovery event, (d) happy-path reconcile writes True with the matching Reason. Same pattern as the existing CredentialKeysRotated/FernetKeysRotated event-condition coupling.
